name: Orange

You'll produce beautiful color variations with Cinnabar without the glaze running off your ware during a cone 6 oxidation firing.

One coat will create a translucent finish with subsequent coats adding opacity. We suggest two to three coats for full opacity and all over coverage. Will fire to a gloss finish without clear glaze. Cone 6 oxidation (smaller image) : Opaque. No color change.

Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Off-white variation darkens.

name: Himalayan Salt
description: Cone 6: Himalayan Salt is a soft matte glaze with light pink and burnt orange mottling that will pool a glossy clear. Cone 10: Color changes to an opaque, variegated gray/white that breaks gray over texture and can pool a glossy purple/blue when thick.

name: Azurite
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image) : Azurite is a soft matte, cobalt blue glaze that will pool dark and glossy and break a light brown. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Color changes to green where thin/breaking and remains dark blue where thick/pooling. Increased movement.

name: Rainforest
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image): Rainforest is a soft matte, emerald green glaze that will pool dark and glossy and break a light brown. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image): Color changes to light brown with green variation where thick/pooling. Increased movement.

name: Riptide
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image): Riptide is a satin-matte, opaque, variegated turquoise/green glaze that breaks over texture and develops a glossy finish where thick or pooling. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image): Glaze shifts grey with white/turquoise variegation. Glaze remains stable.

name: Amber Topaz
description: Cone 6: Amber Topaz provides a translucent, light honey-blonde color. Designed to provide a hint of color with one coat, deeper color with two to three coats but remaining translucent. Cone 10: Color changes to a transparent brown.

name: Nimbus
description: Cone 6 oxidation (large photo): Nimbus is a glossy, opaque, variegated blue glaze that breaks over texture and has an iridescent sheen. Blue micro-crystalization may develop. Cone 10 reduction (small photo): Glaze brightens to a variegated cyan that breaks green/brown over texture.

name: Ivy
description: Cone 6 oxidation (large photo): Ivy is a glossy, opaque, variegated green glaze with that breaks over texture and has an iridescent sheen. Green micro-crystallization may develop. Cone 10 reduction (small photo): Glaze shifts to a variegated jade that breaks purple over texture. Micro-crystallization enhances.

name: Fossil Rock
description: Cone 6 oxidation (large photo): Geologically inspired with mottled earth tones, Fossil Rock is an opaque, tan, matte glaze that breaks over texture. Cone 10 reduction (small photo): Glaze shifts ivory with soft gray and warm brown marbling. Breaks slate gray over texture. Glaze remains stable.

name: Root Beer
description: Cone 6: Root Beer provides translucent, rich, brown gloss color. Designed to provide a hint of color with one coat, deeper color with two to three coats but remaining translucent. Cone 10: Color darkens.

name: Turquoise
description: Cone 6: Turquoise provides translucent, bluish-green gloss color. Designed to provide a hint of color with one coat, deeper color with two to three coats but remaining translucent. Cone 10: Color fades.

name: Rose Quartz
description: Cone 6 oxidation (large photo): Rose quartz is a glossy, opaque, variegated pink glaze that breaks over texture. Pastel pink micro-crystallization may develop on vertical surfaces. Cone 10 reduction (small photo): Glaze darkens and desaturates to a dusty rose with red and mauve variegation and white micro-crystallization. Breaks mauve grey over texture.

name: Celadon Bloom
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image) : Celadon Bloom is a turquoise semi-transparent gloss glaze that contains a white crystal. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Color lightens. Crystals remain.

name: Weathered Blue

name: Wildfire
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Wildfire is an opaque orange base glaze with large yellow crystals that bloom and small black specks. Cone 6 oxidation (small photo): Base glaze remains – crystals fade and melt. Enhanced mobility. Surface issues may occur.

name: Muddy Waters
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image) : Muddy Waters is a matte brown glaze with crystals that melt into a glossy blue. Heavier crystal application will produce more color variation. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Glossy blue becomes more prominent. Increased movement.

name: Black Iris
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Black Iris is an opaque black base glaze with a variety of golden/yellow/brown crystals. Cone 6 oxidation (small photo): Base glaze remains – crystals fade and melt. Enhanced mobility. Surface issues may occur.

name: Wintergreen
description: Cone 6: Wintergreen is a light-green, semi-opaque satin matte glaze at cone 6. Lighter coatings will allow body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Use three coats to achieve the deepest opacity. Cone 10: Changes to a transparent pale green gloss with matte crystal growth.

name: Tree Green
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Produces a beautiful, smooth high dark gloss green finish and can be intermixed for custom colors. Cone 6 oxidation (small photo): No color change.

name: Gray Matte
description: Cone 6: Gray Matte is formulated to produce a smooth matte surface. Light coats will allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Cone 10: Color changes to a blue/gray.

name: Light Blue
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Produces a beautiful, smooth high gloss light blue finish and can be intermixed for custom colors. Cone 6 oxidation (small photo): No color change. Breaks over texture.

name: Black Matte
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image) : Black Matte is formulated to produce a smooth matte surface. Light coats will allow clay body characteristics to appear through the glaze (especially on dark and speckled clay bodies). Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Color darkens. TIP: A true matte finish at cone 5/6 with no movement. Thicker application, three or more coats, will produce a more opaque finish.

name: Lime Shower
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image) : Lime Shower is formulated to produce a vibrant chartreuse gloss glaze with dark green and white crystals. Light coats will produce a semi-transparent finish in the base glaze with fewer crystals. Use three or more coats to produce an opaque green float with more saturation of crystals. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Color becomes less vibrant. Crystals melt.

name: Sahara Sands
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Sahara Sands is a glossy, variegated tan glaze with an orangish brown stain.

name: Moonscape
description: Cone 6: Moonscape is formulated to produce a medium gray to white variegation mottled with black crystals. Light coats will produce a matte gray finish with little white variegation and few crystals. Use three or more coats to produce more white variegation and crystals. Cone 10: Off-white variation darkens. Gray variation turns blue. Crystals melt.

name: Abalone
description: Cone 6 oxidation (larger image) : Abalone is formulated to produce a breaking white and mauve semi-gloss glaze. Light applications will produce a more white semi-gloss finish. Use three+ coats to achieve semi-gloss Abalone finish. Clay body characteristics will be visible through this glaze. Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Color darkens to purple.

name: Holiday Green
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Holiday Green is a glossy, deep yellow-green glaze that breaks a darker green. Semi-Transparent at 3 coats. Not dinnerware safe.

name: Olive Float

name: Sandstone
description: Cone 6: Sandstone is a matte, off-white glaze that contains a variety of brown crystals that melt during the firing. Thicker application and heavy crystal concentration will produce more glossy blue/purple variation. Cone 10: Color darkens. Glossy blue/purples become more prominent.

name: Grass
description: Cone 06 oxidation (large photo): Grass is a glossy, green glaze with an orangish brown stain.

name: Lilac Matte
description: Cone 6: Lilac Matte is a semi-opaque matte lilac glaze. The glaze will break over surface textures. Cone 10: Changes to a translucent gloss.

name: Galaxy

Cone 10 reduction (smaller image) : Crystals and base showcase more blue hues. Crystals melt.

name: Shipwreck

name: Black
description: CR-1 is a glossy black glaze that, when fired, crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms. The result is a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns that add unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The featured chip image of CR-1 is applied on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware. Layering sample images are 2 coats over 2 coats, fired to Cone 6 Apply 1-4 even coats of glaze to cone 04 bisque. Allow each coat to dry almost fully, but before any significant cracking occurs. Crawl glazes will develop hairline cracks as they dry; handle with care. For added durability before firing, layer over an AMACO Celadon glaze or underglaze More coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. Increased coats will produce more cracks. Fire to cone 5-6 for best results These glazes are food safe when layered over AMACO Celadons.

Fantasy Glazes can vary dramatically in appearance depending on the clay body used, the thickness of application, and in some cases, the firing. Anywhere that one of the five overcoats is laid on top of Licorice, Coffee Bean, or Brick Red, a stunning spotted pattern will appear during firing. Thicker applications lead to bigger spots. Try using wax resist after the first layer to create negative space designs.

Coyote Gloss Glazes provide bold, bright, solid colors for your ware. Durable and not runny, these glazes are equally suited to both decorative and practical ware. They combine great with other Coyote glazes, especially the Archie's and Shino series.

name: Iris
description: A translucent purple. Frank's Colored Celadons are a beautiful series of translucent glazes. Because they can be seen through, they are perfect for work with fine details or carving, or as an alternative to clear for ware with underglaze. They do not run and look great on their own or combined with other glazes.

name: Temmoku
description: Temmoku is a textured, satin-matte dark brown/tan speckled glaze that looks great layered beneath some of the more fluid Potter's Choice glazes. With its consistent surface and reliable results, this glaze makes a fantastic liner for cups, pots, bowls, and other ware.

name: Lapis Satin
description: A deep bluish purple with no breaking. Gun Metal Green and Lapis Satin is one of the most popular combinations of glaze, creating something unlike both colors. Our Satin glazes have a soft, opaque surface that feels smooth to the touch. Durable, bright, and reliable, they are the perfect series for any functional potter seeking a contrast from more typical gloss glazes. They are not runny, are easy to use, and look great with the Archie's series.

name: Lemon Cream Satin
description: A bright and unbreaking yellow, similar to Buttercup but without the glossy finish. Our Satin glazes have a soft, opaque surface that feels smooth to the touch. Durable, bright, and reliable, they are the perfect series for any functional potter seeking a contrast from more typical gloss glazes. They are not runny, are easy to use, and look great with the Archie's series.

name: Deep Olive Speckle
description: Deep Olive Speckle is a rich, translucent, glossy green glaze with darker green speckles. Looks great when layered with our other Potter's Choice glazes. This glaze contains black speckles that may clog a spray gun. Make sure glaze is well-mixed for even distribution of speckles.

name: Frosted Turquoise
description: Frosted Turquoise is an opaque gray-blue glaze that appears to crackle over deep blue but has a smooth, satiny surface. This glaze is a great, detailed surface decoration for subtle, simple forms. Frosted Turquoise benefits from a heavy application to achieve its color breaking effect. The glaze color is susceptible to variation due to iron fuming from nearby pots in firing.

name: Midnight Blue
description: DL-12 Midnight Blue is a glossy, deep navy blue glaze that breaks into lighter blue highlights where thick. This glaze is good for textured work and layers well with other DL glazes While measuring the specific gravity is not required to initially mix the AMACO Dipping and Layering glazes, there may be a time you need to know the recommended specific gravity for DL-12 Midnight Blue is 1.52.

name: Tourmaline
description: Tourmaline is an intense, opaque blue-green glaze that forms a smooth, strong surface. Goes well layered with other potters choice glazes!

name: Charcoal Satin
description: A great alternative to Black for potters looking for less shine on their finished work. A natural looking dark charcoal color that absorbs light. Durable, bright, and reliable, they are the perfect series for any functional potter seeking a contrast from more typical gloss glazes. They are not runny, are easy to use, and look great with the Archie's series.

name: Blue Lagoon
description: The cool tones of Blue Lagoon break milky green and pool into light blue shallows when applied over deep texture. When heavier coats are applied this allows the glaze to float up and create interesting surface variations similar to tides in a blue lagoon. A fluid glaze.

name: Ice

name: Archie's Base

Pale pink or sometimes cream colored on its own, the Archie's really shines when combined with other glazes, changing their colors and pulling them into interesting new patterns. The Archie's Series can be challenging to use, but rewards your skill with some of the most beautiful glazes available. They flow and interact with other colors dramatically, creating fantastic finishes unlike anything else possible in an electric kiln. They are also strongly affected by slow-cooling.

name: Sapphire
description: A deep, clear blue. Translucent enough to be used over fine details or underglaze, Sapphire starts as a pale blue and becomes darker as it pools. name: Opal

name: Blue Rutile
description: Great for layering and textured work, Blue Rutile is an active, complex glaze that is a flowing light blue where thick and breaks brown where thinner. Great for layering and always interesting, this glaze is always one of our top selling Potters Choice glazes! A very fluid glaze.

name: Moon River

Glaze layering fired results may run if paired with a runny glaze. We recommend testing any layering combination prior to putting it on a full ceramic piece Shake well before use.

description: This glaze is currently available at your local distributor PC-18 Tuscan Blue is a deep blue, semi-matte glaze that breaks light blue to moss green over texture Layers well over AMACO Celadons, other Potter's Choice and Satin Matte glazes. name: Fire Opal
description: A bright pink with a lovely haze where thick. Fire Opal is great on its own, and combines well with both the Gloss and Archie' Series. name: Periwinkle
description: A pale lavender glaze with a lovely satin finish. Periwinkle should be applied heavily to ensure even coverage, as it can look a bit patchy where thin. They do not run, resist scratching and crazing, and look pretty much the same in all firing cycles.

name: Sundrop
description: Sundrop is a bright and cheery yellow with a hard glossy surface. It resists staining, scratching and crazing, while providing a warm vibrance to any piece. Vibro-Colors are as stable and reliable as Enduro-Color Liner Glazes, but loaded up with colorant for a vibrant pop. They do not run, resist scratching and crazing, and look pretty much the same in all firing cycles.

name: Passion Red
description: A vivid red with a smooth glossy surface. Vibrant, beautiful, and easy to use, it's quickly becoming a Coyote favorite. Vibro-Colors are as stable and reliable as Enduro-Color Liner Glazes, but loaded up with colorant for a vibrant pop. They do not run, resist scratching and crazing, and look pretty much the same in all firing cycles.

name: Satin Oribe
description: A modern take on a traditional Japanese oribe glaze, Satin Oribe adds a wonderful blue-green burnished look to your wares. Over texture, the dark green pools and light grey breaks bring an extra level of depth to this opaque glaze.

name: Chun Plum
description: Chun Plum is a pink-red translucent glaze that breaks light tan over textures. This glaze imitates the copper reds of Asia but with a reliable color response in oxidation firings. Layer over other Potters Choice glazes for a warmer tone and hints of pink and red.

name: Antares
description: A deep, intense blue. One of the most dramatic of the Constellation glazes, Antares will develop starry crystals and shades of bronze as it is cooled more slowly. The Constellations are the first line of glazes designed to shine in both regular and slow-cooled firings. Thickness, firing temperature, and especially cooling speed can affect these glazes, ensuring they are never the same twice.

name: Firefly Garden
description: Fantasy Glazes can vary dramatically in appearance depending on the clay body used, the thickness of application, and in some cases, the firing. They can be runny, so it's important to test carefully.

name: Tiger Lily
description: Tiger Lily is a bright and cheery orange with a hard glossy surface. It resists staining, scratching and crazing, while providing a warm vibrance to any piece. Vibro-Colors are as stable and reliable as Enduro-Color Liner Glazes, but loaded up with colorant for a vibrant pop. They do not run, resist scratching and crazing, and look pretty much the same in all firing cycles.

name: Sea Mist
description: Sea Mist is one of the Texas Two Step Overcoats, meaning it can be layered with Licorice, Coffee Bean, or Brick Red to produce an oilspot effect. When used alone, it is a mottled blue-green that can develop pale spots on its own. The Texas Two-Step Oilspots are a specialized series that use pairs of glazes to create unique effects. Anywhere that one of the five overcoats is laid on top of Licorice, Coffee Bean, or Brick Red, a stunning spotted pattern will appear during firing. Thicker applications lead to bigger spots. Try using wax resist after the first layer to create negative space designs.

name: Marigold
description: This 100% mixable celadon glaze is a glossy, deep yellow that pools and accents textured ware like the ancient glazes it is created to imitate.

name: Dragon's Eye
description: Fantasy Glazes can vary dramatically in appearance depending on the clay body used, the thickness of application, and in some cases, the firing. They can be runny, so it's important to test carefully.

name: Gemini
description: A brown glaze with blue tones when cooled quickly, and bronze tones when slow cooled. Looks vastly changed on different clays and at different thicknesses. The Constellations are the first line of glazes designed to shine in both regular and slow-cooled firings. Thickness, firing temperature, and especially cooling speed can affect these glazes, ensuring they are never the same twice.

name: Smokey Merlot

name: Clementine
description: Clementine is a bright orange that adds a warm pop to any palette. The Texas Two-Step Oilspots are a specialized series that use pairs of glazes to create unique effects. The Texas Two-Step Oilspots are a specialized series that use pairs of glazes to create unique effects. Anywhere that one of the six overcoats is laid on top of an undercoat, a stunning spotted pattern will appear during firing. Thicker applications lead to bigger spots. Try using wax resist after the first layer to create negative space designs.

name: Speckled Teal
description: CR-22 is a glossy teal glaze with black specks that, when fired, crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms. The result is a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns that add unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The featured chip image of CR-22 is applied on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware. Layering Sample Images are 2 coats over 2 coats, fired to Cone 6 Apply 1-4 even coats of glaze to cone 04 bisque. Allow each coat to dry almost fully, but before any significant cracking occurs. Crawl glazes will develop hairline cracks as they dry; handle with care. For added durability before firing, layer over an AMACO Celadon glaze or underglaze More coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. Increased coats will produce more cracks. Fire to cone 5-6 for best results These glazes are food safe when layered over AMACO Celadons.

name: Pumpkin
description: Pumpkin is a vivid, opaque orange-colored glaze that boasts a glossy, smooth surface after firing. 100% mixable and true to their unfired color, the Teachers Palette glazes are a must-have for any classroom or studio. Also try using using Cotton (TP-11) as a base glaze and, using any of the other Teacher's Palette colors, brush on colorful designs and patterns reminiscent of majolica ware.

name: Clementine
description: Clementine is a bright orange that adds a warm pop to any palette. It is also a Texas Two Step Overcoat, which can be layered with one of the six Two Step Undercoats to produce an oilspot effect. The Texas Two-Step Oilspots are a specialized series that use pairs of glazes to create unique effects. Anywhere that one of the five overcoats is laid on top of Licorice, Coffee Bean, or Brick Red, a stunning spotted pattern will appear during firing. Thicker applications lead to bigger spots. Try using wax resist after the first layer to create negative space designs.

name: Mediterranean Sea
description: A beautiful glossy blue with a stain resistant finish that looks especially bright on white clays like B-Mix. Perfect for dishes, vases, tiles, and anything else. Vibro-Colors are as stable and reliable as Enduro-Color Liner Glazes, but loaded up with colorant for a vibrant pop. They do not run, resist scratching and crazing, and look pretty much the same in all firing cycles.

name: Passion Red
description: A vivid red with a smooth glossy surface. Not only is Passion Red scratch resistant, stain resistant, and craze resistant, it's also less prone to the pinhole problem that affects many other red glazes. Vibrant, beautiful, and easy to use, it's quickly becoming a Coyote favorite. Vibro-Colors are as stable and reliable as Enduro-Color Liner Glazes, but loaded up with colorant for a vibrant pop. They do not run, resist scratching and crazing, and look pretty much the same in all firing cycles.

name: Periwinkle
description: A pale lavender glaze with a lovely satin finish. Periwinkle should be applied heavily to ensure even coverage, as it can look a bit patchy where thin. description: Unicorn Horn is one of the most versatile and surprising glazes we have ever made. Varying from pearlescent white, to mottled ivory, to ancient yellow, this magical glaze is perfect for buff clays with lots of texture. Fantasy Glazes can vary dramatically in appearance depending on the clay body used, the thickness of application, and in some cases, the firing. They can be runny, so it's important to test carefully.

name: Phoenix Egg
description: A translucent brown with mesmerizing rivulets, Phoenix Egg can display a dazzling array of colors. Under the right circumstances, amber, coffee, purple and blue tones can show across a surface, and gold crystals can even form in the areas where the glaze cools slowest. Fantasy Glazes can vary dramatically in appearance depending on the clay body used, the thickness of application, and in some cases, the firing. They can be runny, so it's important to test carefully.

name: Cherry Satin
description: A bright, true red. Similar to the Really Red glaze, but with a less glossy finish that absorbs the light. Our Satin glazes have a soft, opaque surface that feels smooth to the touch. Durable, bright, and reliable, they are the perfect series for any functional potter seeking a contrast from more typical gloss glazes. They are not runny, are easy to use, and look great with the Archie's series.

name: Texas Rose
description: Texas Rose is a lovely reddish pink, with an interesting mottling to the surface that can only be seen up close. It is also a Texas Two Step Overcoat, which can be layered with Licorice, Coffee Bean, or Brick Red to produce an oilspot effect. The Texas Two-Step Oilspots are a specialized series that use pairs of glazes to create unique effects. Anywhere that one of the five overcoats is laid on top of Licorice, Coffee Bean, or Brick Red, a stunning spotted pattern will appear during firing. Thicker applications lead to bigger spots. Try using wax resist after the first layer to create negative space designs.

name: Flame Orange Underglaze
description: Flame Orange Velvet Underglaze is a carrot color that is a bright red-orange at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes a deep persimmon color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Flame Orange Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-10 Clear. HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Intense Yellow Underglaze
description: Intense Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a bright pineapple color that is vivid at cone 05, deepens at cone 5, and is the deepest and most intense at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Intense Yellow Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-10 Clear. HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Blood Orange Underglaze
description: Blood Orange is a fiery tangerine color at Cone 05 which intensifies under a clear glaze. When fired to Cone 5 and Cone 10, it becomes slightly darker.

name: Red Brown Underglaze
description: Red Brown Velvet Underglaze is a warm cocoa color that is reddish at cone 05. The color darkens at cone 5, and becomes chocolatey at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Chocolate Brown Underglaze
description: Chocolate Brown Velvet Underglaze is a deep brown that is warmest at cone 05. The color darkens at cone 5, and is darkest but less saturated at cone 10. This underglaze looks great beneath a few coats of a clear glaze.

name: Peach Underglaze
description: Peach Velvet Underglaze is a warm pink at cone 05 that darkens slightly at cone 5, and becomes a light coral at cone 10. A clear glaze darkens the color slightly at any temperature Peach Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under C-11 Mixing Clear Originally formulated to dilute the color of Celadon series glazes, C-11 Mixing Clear can be used as a clear glaze. Mixing Clear is a stiff gloss glaze that works well with most underglazes.

name: Coral Underglaze
description: Coral is a warm, bright pink underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 10 with minimal darkening. Clear glaze brightens the color. Can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.

name: Rose Underglaze
description: Rose Velvet underglaze is a deep magenta that is dark at cone 05, becomes patchy at cone 5 before it burns out almost completely at cone 10. A clear glaze over Rose will accentuate the color at cone 05 but at cone 5 and 10, there is no color change between glazed and unglazed.

name: Lavender Underglaze
description: Lavender velvet underglaze is a periwinkle color that is soft at cone 05, becomes deeper at cone 5, and burns out at cone 10. A clear glaze over Lavender will accentuate the color at cone 05 and 5 and preserves some bluish tones from the underglaze at cone 10.

name: Lilac Underglaze
description: Lilac velvet underglaze is a soft plum color that is light at cone 05 that becomes warmer and deeper at cone 5 and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze over Lilac will accentuate the color at cone 05 and 5 and will burn out a lot of the color at cone 10.

name: Purple Underglaze
description: Purple Velvet underglaze is a violet color that is soft at cone 05, becomes bluish and lighter at cone 5 before it becomes very light and blue at cone 10. A clear glaze over Purple will accentuate the color at cone 05 and cone 5 and causes more blue tones at cone 10.

name: Salmon Underglaze
description: Salmon Velvet underglaze is a soft peach color that is light at cone 05, becomes warmer and darker at cone 5, and maintains a toasted peach color at cone 10. A clear glaze over Salmon will accentuate and darken the color at cone 05, cone 5, and cone 10.

name: Blush Underglaze
description: Blush is a warm, soft pink underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 10 with minimal darkening. Clear glaze brightens the color. Blush underglaze can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.

name: Baby Blue Underglaze
description: Baby Blue Velvet underglaze is a soft light blue color that is bright and pastel at cone 05, becomes darker at cone 5, and is darker sky blue at cone 10. A clear glaze over Baby blue will accentuate the color at cone 05 but begins to burn out some of the color at cone 5 and cone 10.

name: Medium Blue Underglaze
description: Medium Blue Velvet Underglaze is a brilliant primary blue that is vivid at cone 05, darker at cone 5, maintaining its blue color. A clear glaze over Medium Blue will significantly darken the color at any temperature.

name: Turquoise Blue Underglaze
description: Turquoise Blue Velvet underglaze is a blue green color that is cyan at cone 05, becomes deeper cone 5, and becomes very dark at cone 10. This underglaze maintains its color well under a clear glaze at any temperature.

name: Iceberg Blue Underglaze
description: Iceberg Blue Velvet underglaze is a light blue color that is powder blue at cone 05, becomes slightly darker at cone 5, and maintains its light blue shade up to cone 10. A clear glaze over Iceberg Blue tends to lift the color out slightly, lightening it.

name: Sea Glass Underglaze
description: Sea Glass is a very pale blue-green color at cone 05 and becomes richer and darker under a clear glaze. It remains the same at cone 5, but fired to cone 10 becomes more blue.

name: Indigo Underglaze
description: Indigo is a deep purple-blue color at cone 05 and darkens under a clear glaze. When fired to cone 5, it becomes more blue in color and then darkens slightly at cone 10.

name: Teal Blue Underglaze
description: Teal Blue underglaze is a deep blue color that is rich sapphire at cone 05 that becomes a deep medium blue at cone 5, and is very dark at cone 10. A clear glaze over Teal Blue will drastically darken its color at any temperature.

name: Avocado Underglaze
description: Avocado Velvet Underglaze is a pea green color that is vivid at cone 05, darkens at cone 5 and becomes a deep chartreuse at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Avocado Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Royal Blue Underglaze
description: Royal Blue Velvet Underglaze is a navy blue that is deep and saturated at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes almost black at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Blue Green Underglaze
description: Blue Green Velvet Underglaze is a teal color that is deep aqua at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes a dark marine blue at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Chartreuse Underglaze
description: Chartreuse Velvet Underglaze is a lime green color that is very bright at cone 05, softens to a mint green at cone 5, and darkens slightly at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Light Green Underglaze
description: Light Green Velvet Underglaze is a light spring green color that is bright at cone 05, darkens significantly to a forest green at cone 5, and becomes a deep pine green at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze at any temperature.

name: Pistachio Underglaze
description: Pistachio is a light green underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 6 with minimal darkening. Above cone 6 the color becomes slightly bluer. Clear glaze brightens the color. Pistachio underglaze can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.

name: Cactus Underglaze
description: Cactus is a cool light green underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 6 with minimal darkening. Above cone 6 the color becomes slightly bluer. Clear glaze brightens the color. Cactus underglaze can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.

name: Orange Underglaze
description: Orange Velvet Underglaze is a golden color that is bright at cone 05, softens to a toasty beige at cone 5, and becomes toastier at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Dark Green Underglaze
description: Dark Green Velvet Underglaze is a rich green color that is bright and saturated at cone 05, darkens to a forest green at cone 5, and becomes slightly darker at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Dark Green Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Pearl Gray Underglaze
description: Pearl Gray Velvet Underglaze is a bluish silver gray color that is bright at cone 05, darkens to a pewter tone at cone 5 and begins to lighten and burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Ultra White Underglaze
description: Ultra White Velvet Underglaze is a bright and opaque white at cone 05, a frosty color at cone 5, and maintains its vividness at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: White Underglaze
description: White Velvet Underglaze is snow white color that is bright and minty at cone 05, softens at cone 5, and becomes slight warmer, but maintains a clean white color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze at any temperature.

name: Jet Black Underglaze
description: Jet Black Velvet Underglaze is a cool black color that is smooth and velvety at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes darkest black and slightly glossy at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens and accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature Jet Black Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under SM-10 Clear Satin SM-10 Clear Satin is a silky, stiff zinc-free clear glaze that works well as a liner or over underglazes. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.

name: Ultra Black Underglaze
description: Ultra Black Velvet Underglaze fires to a beautiful soft, dark matte color at cone 05 then deepens to a darker black at cone 5 and maintains it’s shade at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature Ultra Black Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under SM-10 Clear Satin SM-10 Clear Satin is a silky, stiff zinc-free clear glaze that works well as a liner or over underglazes. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.

name: Morning Fog Underglaze
description: Morning Fog Velvet Underglaze fires to a light, misty gray when fired to cone 05, darkens to a steamy color at cone 5, and maintains the foggy color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Elephant Underglaze
description: Elephant Velvet Underglaze is a light, ashy shade when fired to cone 05, darkens to a deeper gray at cone 5 then turns to a grayish-black akin to elephant skin when fired to cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Slate Underglaze
description: Slate Velvet Underglaze is a dark, bluish-gray at cone 05 that darkens to a deep, slatey shade when fired to cone 5 and transforms into a melancholic blue-green at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Teddy Bear Brown
description: Teddy Bear Velvet Underglaze is a chestnut color that is a warm tan at cone 05, darkens to a russet color at cone 5, and lightens and begins to burn out cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze at cone 05 but burns out some of the color at cone 5 and cone 10 For Teddy Bear Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Velour Black Underglaze
description: Velour Black Velvet Underglaze is a warm black color that is deep and velvety at cone 05, darkens and becomes warmer at cone 5, and is silky and deep at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature Velour Black Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under SM-10 Clear Satin SM-10 Clear Satin is a silky, stiff zinc-free clear glaze that works well as a liner or over underglazes. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.

name: Maroon Underglaze
description: Maroon Velvet Underglaze is a deep burgundy color that is soft and rich at cone 05, deepens at cone 5, and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at cone 05 and intensifies its rich oxblood shade at cone 5.

name: Hunter Green Underglaze
description: Hunter Green Velvet Underglaze is an emerald color that is vivid at cone 05, darkens to a deep blue-green at cone 5, and becomes slightly darker and develops a sheen at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Hunter Green Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Ultra Violet Underglaze
description: Ultra Violet Velvet Underglaze is a soft, pinkish-purple at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5, then lightens when fired to cone 10 but still maintains its violet shade. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Violet Underglaze
description: Violet Velvet Underglaze is a light plum color that is soft at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes a deep lilac color and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze nicely at any temperature.

name: Amethyst Underglaze
description: Amethyst Velvet Underglaze is a deep grape color that is vivid at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes a deep violet and begins to lose its color slightly at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Red Underglaze
description: Red Velvet Underglaze is a crimson that is vivid at cone 05, deepens to a dark wine red at cone 5, and becomes brownish and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze nicely at cone 05 and cone 5.

name: Light Red Underglaze
description: Light Red Velvet Underglaze is a light coral red that is bright at cone 05, softens slightly at cone 5 and becomes darker and slightly glossy at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color nicely at any temperature.

name: Real Orange Underglaze
description: Real Orange Velvet Underglaze is a luminous orange color that is bright and saturated at cone 05, softens and becomes lighter at cone 5 and becomes slightly warmer and is still bright at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Cinnamon Underglaze
description: Cinnamon Velvet Underglaze is a deep sienna color that is very warm at cone 05, darkens and becomes chocolatey at cone 5, and becomes a dark brown at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Electric Blue Underglaze
description: Electric Blue Velvet Underglaze is a very deep cobalt blue that is vivid at cone 05, darkens significantly at cone 5, and becomes a very dark navy blue at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.

name: Bright Red Underglaze
description: Bright Red Velvet Underglaze is a deep crimson color that is vivid at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5, and becomes a deep cherry red at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Bright Red Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-10 Clear. HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Radiant Red Underglaze
description: Radiant Red Velvet Underglaze is a bright scarlet color that is vivid at cone 05, deepens at cone 5, and becomes slightly glossy and a deep fire-engine red at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Radiant Red Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-10 Clear. HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Bright Orange Underglaze
description: Bright Orange Velvet Underglaze is a tangerine color that is neon at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5 and becomes a deep pumpkin at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature For Bright Orange Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-10 Clear. HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Black
description: Black Liquid Underglaze is a deep obsidian black that is dark and smooth at cone 05, fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains a deep black tone with a soft sheen all the way to cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Black an impeccably smooth finish with relatively good color, though it can cause small moments of transparency in the surface.

name: White
description: White Liquid Underglaze is a snow white that is light and dry at cone 05, a beautiful, slightly-warm white that fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and a slightly brighter white with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give White an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Warm Gray
description: Warm Gray Liquid Underglaze is a soft gray that is light and smoky at cone 05, becomes deeper gray and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains a slate gray tone with a soft sheen all the way to cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Warm Gray an impeccably smooth surface with good color For Warm Gray Liquid Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Light Blue
description: Light Blue Liquid Underglaze is a pastel blue that is dry and soft at cone 05, becomes deeper in color and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains a sky blue tone with a soft sheen all the way to cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Light Blue an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Medium Blue
description: Medium Blue Liquid Underglaze is an electric blue color that is soft and bright at cone 05, becomes more intense and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains a strong blue tone with a soft sheen all the way to cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Medium Blue an impeccably smooth finish with good color at cone 05 and cone 6, but tends to cause some transparency in the surface at cone 10.

name: Dark Blue
description: Dark Blue Liquid Underglaze is a deep navy that is a dark cobalt at cone 05, becomes a soft midnight blue and fluxes into a glaze-like surface at cone 6, and becomes almost black with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Dark Blue an impeccably smooth surface and intensify its color.

name: Turquoise
description: Turquoise Liquid Underglaze is a light teal color that is pastel and light at cone 05, becomes deeper teal and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains a blue green tone with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Turquoise an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Aqua
description: Aqua Liquid Underglaze is a soft blue green that is very pastel at cone 05, becomes a much deeper blue green while fluxing into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and ends up losing a lot of color at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Aqua an impeccably smooth surface with good color at cone 05 and cone 6, but can burn out some of the color at cone 10.

name: Light Brown
description: Light Brown Liquid Underglaze is a light chestnut color that is warm and light at cone 05, becomes a warm tawny color and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains this warm tan tone with a soft sheen all the way to cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Light Brown an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Chartreuse
description: Chartreuse Liquid Underglaze is a lime green color that is bright at cone 05, becomes slightly darker and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and softens to a mint green with a soft sheen at to cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Chartreuse an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Warm Green
description: Warm Green Liquid Underglaze is a soft green that is light and minty at cone 05, becomes a beautiful, creamy shamrock color while fluxing into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and darkens greatly to a forest green with a light gloss at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Warm Green an impeccably smooth surface with excellent color.

name: Blue Green
description: Blue Green Liquid Underglaze is a deep teal that is soft and light at cone 05, becomes a much deeper blue green and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains that deeper tone with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Blue Green an impeccably smooth surface with good color For Blue Green Liquid Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Dark Green
description: Dark Green Liquid Underglaze is a leaf green color that is light and vivid at cone 05, becomes deeper green and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and becomes a kelly green color with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Dark Green an impeccably smooth surface with good color For Dark Green Liquid Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.

name: Pink
description: Pink Liquid Underglaze is a cherry blossom pink that is a light blush color at cone 05, becomes warmer pink and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and is a warm salmon pink with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Pink an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Rose
description: Rose Liquid Underglaze is a soft primrose color that is light and soft at cone 05, becomes a deep raspberry and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and burns out almost completely at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Rose an impeccably smooth surface with good color at cone 05 and cone 6.

name: Peach
description: Peach Liquid Underglaze is a light pink that is dry at cone 05, becomes more of a nude tone and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains a fleshy tone with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Peach an impeccably smooth surface while altering the color slightly, causing more of a beige tone to occur.

name: Suntan
description: Suntan Liquid Underglaze is a soft tan color that is light and soft at cone 05, becoming more creamy at cone 6 and cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Suntan an impeccably smooth surface while altering the color slightly.

name: Lilac
description: Lilac Liquid Underglaze is a soft orchid purple that is light and dry at cone 05, becomes slightly deeper and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and begins to burn out at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Lilac an impeccably smooth surface and can help preserve some of the color at cone 10.

name: Purple
description: Purple Liquid Underglaze is a soft purple color that is light and dry at cone 05, becomes more of a creamy light blue and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and begins to burn out at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Purple an impeccably smooth surface with intensified color, but will cause more color loss at higher temperatures.

name: Maroon
description: Maroon Liquid Underglaze is a plum color that is a light burgundy at cone 05, becomes a beautiful, deep mauve and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and begins to burn out at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Maroon an impeccably smooth surface with good color, but will cause more color loss at higher temperatures.

name: Dark Red
description: Dark Red Liquid Underglaze is crimson color that is light and dry at cone 05, becomes a beautiful, deep red and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and darkens slightly while maintaining a soft sheen at cone 10. The underglaze does become glaze-like, but with good color.

name: Light Yellow
description: Light Yellow Liquid Underglaze is a light daffodil yellow that is pale and dry at cone 05, becomes a deep lemony yellow and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and lightens slightly at cone 10 with some slight pinholing. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Light Yellow an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Bright Yellow
description: Bright Yellow Liquid Underglaze is a lemon yellow that is soft and bright at cone 05, becomes a bright maize color and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and lightens slightly while maintaining a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Bright Yellow an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Orange
description: Orange Liquid Underglaze is a tangerine color that is bright at cone 05, becomes a terra cotta red and fluxes into a soft glaze-like surface at cone 6, and maintains that toasted red-orange tone with a soft sheen at cone 10. Though the underglaze does become glaze-like, a clear gloss glaze can give Orange an impeccably smooth surface with good color.

name: Onyx
description: Onyx is a translucent, coal black glaze that boasts a glossy, smooth surface after firing. 100% mixable and true to their unfired color, the Teacher’s Palette Light® glazes are a must-have for any classroom or studio. These glazes look especially beautiful over carved and textured surfaces. In need of a fun project? Try brushing one layer of any of the Teacher’s Palette Light colors over a design created with one of our Velvet Underglazes.

name: Quartz
description: Quartz is a translucent, storm gray glaze that boasts a glossy, smooth surface after firing. 100% mixable and true to their unfired color, the Teacher’s Palette Light® glazes are a must-have for any classroom or studio. These glazes look especially beautiful over carved and textured surfaces. In need of a fun project? Try brushing one layer of any of the Teacher’s Palette Light colors over a design created with one of our Velvet Underglazes.

name: Aquamarine
description: Aquamarine is a translucent, seafoam green glaze that boasts a glossy, smooth surface after firing. 100% mixable and true to their unfired color, the Teacher’s Palette Light® glazes are a must-have for any classroom or studio. These glazes look especially beautiful over carved and textured surfaces. In need of a fun project? Try brushing one layer of any of the Teacher’s Palette Light colors over a design created with one of our Velvet Underglazes.

name: Zircon
description: Zircon is a translucent, sky blue glaze that boasts a glossy, smooth surface after firing. 100% mixable and true to their unfired color, the Teacher’s Palette Light® glazes are a must-have for any classroom or studio. These glazes look especially beautiful over carved and textured surfaces. In need of a fun project? Try brushing one layer of any of the Teacher’s Palette Light colors over a design created with one of our Velvet Underglazes.

name: Yellow
description: VS-308 Yellow Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a bright lemon yellow that is vivid at cone 05. It is still bright, but less saturated at cone 05 and becomes pale at cone 10. A clear glaze over Yellow darkens the color slightly, but has less of an effect at higher temperatures Velvet Sprayz are available in 10oz cans The Velvet Sprayz are currently only available to ship within the contiguous U.S.

name: Medium Blue
description: VS-326 Medium Blue Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a brilliant primary blue that is vivid at cone 05, darker at cone 5, maintaining its blue color. A clear glaze over Medium Blue will significantly darken the color at any temperature Velvet Sprayz are available in 10oz cans The Velvet Sprayz are currently only available to ship within the contiguous U.S.

name: Dark Green
description: VS-353 Dark Green Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a rich green color that is bright and saturated at cone 05, darkens to a forest green at cone 5, and becomes slightly darker at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature Velvet Sprayz are available in 10oz cans. The Velvet Sprayz are currently only available to ship within the contiguous U.S.

name: Ultra White
description: VS-359 Ultra White Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a bright and opaque white at cone 05, a frosty color at cone 5, and maintains its vividness at cone 10. The Velvet Sprayz are currently only available to ship within the contiguous U.S.

name: Jet Black
description: VS-361 Jet Black Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a cool black color that is smooth and velvety at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes darkest black and slightly glossy at cone 10. The Velvet Sprayz are currently only available to ship within the contiguous U.S.

name: Real Orange
description: VS-384 Real Orange Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a luminous orange color that is bright and saturated at cone 05, softens and becomes lighter at cone 5 and becomes slightly warmer and is still bright at cone 10. name: Bright Red
description: VS-387 Bright Red Velvet Sprayz underglaze is a deep crimson color that is vivid at cone 05, darkens slightly at cone 5, and becomes a deep cherry red at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color and accentuates the underglaze nicely at any temperature Velvet Sprayz are available in 10oz cans The Velvet Sprayz are currently only available to ship within the contiguous U.S.

name: Ivory Beige Underglaze
description: Ivory Beige Velvet Underglaze is a soft cream color that is very light at cone 05. It warms up a little at cone 5, but burns out almost completely at cone 10. A clear glaze over Ivory Beige accentuates and darkens the color at cone 05 and cone 5. At cone 10, there is no color change between glazed and unglazed.

name: Terra Cotta Underglazes
description: Terra Cotta Velvet Underglaze is a warm cinnamon brown that is much darker with a clear glaze on top at any temperature. At cone 05, it is red-brown and reminiscent of the clay it is named after. It darkens at cone 5 and becomes a deep chocolate color at cone 10.

name: Straw Underglaze
description: Straw Velvet Underglaze is a rich sunflower yellow that is a strong ochre at cone 05 and becomes lighter at cone 5. At cone 10, it is even lighter and less saturated. Overall, it maintains its color well at any temperature and becomes richer and darker under a clear glaze.

name: Yellow Underglaze
description: Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a bright lemon yellow that is vivid at cone 05. It is still bright, but less saturated at cone 05 and becomes pale at cone 10. A clear glaze over Yellow darkens the color slightly, but has less of an effect at higher temperatures.

name: Deep Yellow Underglaze
description: Deep Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a rich ochre color that is very rich at cone 05 and is intense under a clear glaze. At cone 5, it is less saturated and becomes muted and darker at cone 10.

name: Nectar Underglaze
description: Nectar underglaze is a warm yellow color like honeysuckle flowers at cone 05 and becomes more light amber under a clear glaze. It slightly intensifies at cone 5 and cone 10.

name: Set #112
description: Set #112 includes the Red, Bright Yellow, Chartreuse, Warm Green, Aqua, Medium Blue, White, and Black colors of the Semi-Moist Underglazes. The SMUG's are formulated to respond similarly to being brushed like watercolors- becoming more opaque with layered application and more transparent after water is added. They can be blended and diluted to achieve a wide variety of effects Rated for Cone 04-10. *excluding Peach and Rose (Cone 04-6) Must be used with proper glaze to be food safe Cover with a clear transparent glaze or try colored transparent glazes for unique effects. Depending on the clay body used, the clear AMACO® glazes LG-10 and HF-9 may be selected for excellent results.

Official product page notes the line fires from Cone 05 through Cone 10, is intermixable, and is dinnerware-safe when

name: Texture Oasis
description: These reactive glazes are all lead free, non-toxic and dinnerware safe. They are formulated to produce reactions during the firing cycle which give very interesting and beautiful effects. The final appearance of the glaze is dependent on firing temperature, glaze thickness and the composition of the clay body being used. The same glaze can look quite different on different clay bodies. The samples shown on the color charts are 3 brush coats of glaze fired to cone 5 in an electric kiln on a white

description: A beautiful selection of lead free, A/P non-toxic, dinnerware safe stoneware glazes with particular emphasis on bright primary colors

name: Clear Crackle
description: 1187 – Clear Crackle Glaze (cone 4/6) : A lead free, A/P non-toxic clear crackle glaze. After the piece has been removed from the kiln and allowed to cool the crackle pattern will form. A colored dye can be rubbed into the surface to emphasize the crackle pattern. Crackle glazes are not considered dinnerware safe due to the possibility of bacteria growth in the cracks

name: Holly Green
description: These reactive glazes are all lead free but they exceed the vanadium and/or copper threshold limit in the liquid un-fired state and therefore are not non-toxic. However, they are all food safe if fired to the proper firing temperature. They behave in the manner described above for Reactive Glazes. Also be aware that some of the colors, such as 1148, 1152, 1153, 1154, 1158, 1159, 1161, 1162, and particularly 1173 are more fluid and may run. Be more careful when using them on vertical surfaces. Us

name: Gold
description: These metallic glazes have beautiful metallic finishes. All metallic glazes require heavy application; 3-4 thick coats. They are all lead-free but exceed either one or more of the copper, zinc, and manganese threshold limits in the liquid un-fired state and therefore are not non-toxic. However, they are all food safe if fired to the proper firing temperature

name: Milk & Honey
description: These reactive glazes are all lead free, non-toxic and dinnerware safe. They are formulated to produce reactions during the firing cycle which give very interesting and beautiful effects. The final appearance of the glaze is dependent on firing temperature, glaze thickness and the composition of the clay body being used. The same glaze can look quite different on different clay bodies. The samples shown on the color charts are 3 brush coats of glaze fired to cone 5 in an electric kiln on a white

name: Gold Rain
description: These metallic glazes have beautiful metallic finishes. All metallic glazes require heavy application; 3-4 thick coats. They are all lead-free but exceed either one or more of the copper, zinc, and manganese threshold limits in the liquid un-fired state and therefore are not non-toxic. However, they are all food safe if fired to the proper firing temperature

name: White
description: A beautiful selection of lead free, A/P non-toxic, dinnerware safe stoneware glazes with particular emphasis on bright primary colors

name: Satin Clear
description: 1120 – Clear Satin Glaze (cone 4/6): Also lead free, A/P non-toxic, dinnerware safe and suitable for use over underglaze decoration. Satin clear glazes are not as translucent as clear gloss glazes and will tend to mute the appearance of underglaze decoration somewhat. It should be kept thin (i.e. 1 or 2 coats by brushing) to improve clarity

name: Satin White
description: Smooth satin finish on these stoneware glazes which are also lead free, A/P non-toxic and dinnerware safe

name: Texture Burgundy
description: These reactive glazes are all lead free but they exceed the vanadium and/or copper threshold limit in the liquid un-fired state and therefore are not non-toxic. However, they are all food safe if fired to the proper firing temperature. They behave in the manner described above for Reactive Glazes. Also be aware that some of the colors, such as 1148, 1152, 1153, 1154, 1158, 1159, 1161, 1162, and particularly 1173 are more fluid and may run. Be more careful when using them on vertical surfaces. Us

Now the entire range of (48) colors features the same rich color palette in a smooth consistency perfect for any application, no modification required. Official product page notes the line fires from Cone 05 through Cone 10, is intermixable, and is dinnerware-safe when

description: Speedball Mid-Fire Glazes, created in collaboration with ceramic artist Nancy Sowder, are available in an array of nature-inspired colors that yield gorgeous mottling effects and a reduction-like look when fired in a standard oxidation kiln at Cone 5-6. name: Orchid
description: Cone 4/6 celadon or semi-transparent glaze with soft, translucent color. Lead free, dinnerware safe.

name: Cranberry
description: Cone 4/6 celadon or semi-transparent glaze with soft, translucent color. Lead free, dinnerware safe.

name: Mimosa
description: Cone 4/6 celadon or semi-transparent glaze with soft, translucent color. Lead free, dinnerware safe.

name: Bottle Green
description: Cone 4/6 celadon or semi-transparent glaze with soft, translucent color. Lead free, dinnerware safe.

name: Louisiana Bayou
description: Cone 4/6 floating glaze with rich, layered color effects. These glazes produce beautiful surfaces where colors float and separate during firing. Lead free, dinnerware safe.

name: Aurora Green
description: Stoneware crystal glaze producing mottled aqua gloss glaze with light brown and large black crystals at cone 6; shifts to variegated pink/blue/green at cone 10.

name: Indigo Rain
description: Matte white glaze that contains a two-toned purple and violet crystal. At Cone 6, crystals create a subtle effect. At Cone 10, the color darkens to light gray/purple tones as crystals melt.

name: Winter Wood
description: A matte white stoneware crystal glaze with brown crystals that melt during firing. Cone 6 oxidation: Matte white with melting brown crystals. Cone 10 reduction: Translucent golden brown.

name: Norse Blue
description: Semi-opaque blue gloss glaze that displays shades of light blue and green. Variation depends on application thickness. Thinner applications break translucent, while thicker coats appear more homogenous.

name: Stoned Denim
description: Variegated blue glaze that breaks brown. Thinner application yields more brown; thicker application produces more blue.

name: Tiger's Eye
description: Breaks from a glossy deep brownish-red to frothy amber depending on application thickness. One to two coats produce browns and reds; three to five coats result in more glaze movement and color variation.

name: Copper Float
description: Rich and complex midrange glaze with hues varying from reds, to coppers to gun metal blacks. Predominate coloration is dark rusty red. Surface is satin-matte when fired to cone 5 or 6. Finish flattens at cone 10, color changes to warm brown with copper mottling.

name: Lavender Mist
description: A semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of lavender and brown. Thicker applications yield more opaque, uniform color.

name: Sea Green
description: A glossy, blueish teal glaze that breaks dark blueish teal at three coats at Cone 06. At Cone 6, transforms to semi-translucent, variegated olive green/blue. The glaze has significant mobility.

name: Envision Clear
description: Duncan Envision Clear glazes are made to give consistently true-color finishes and outstanding brushability for ease of application. Provides a sparkling, gloss protective finish. Overglaze compatible.

name: Pure Brilliance Clear Dipping
description: A high-performance gloss clear dipping glaze featuring fast and stable dry time and craze-resistant properties. Enhances underglaze colors with brilliant shine and superior protection. Dinnerware safe.

name: Crystal Clear Dipping
description: Preserve and enhance the surface of your work with Mayco Dipping Clear Glazes. Cone 06 oxidation provides clean, clear surface. Cone 6: blistering/pinholing may occur.

name: Clear One Dipping
description: Fast drying dipping clear glaze with gloss finish. Compatible with Chrome Tin pigments and luster. Preserves and enhances ceramic surfaces.

name: Satin Clear
description: Produces a smooth, opaque matte clear finish with only two coats. Can be successfully used over opaque and translucent underglazes. Dinnerware safe.

name: Blue Grotto
description: A glossy, denim blue glaze with a dark gray stain. Designed to simulate mid-range glazes at low-fire temperatures. It moves readily, so application thickness should be reduced toward the bottom of vertical pieces.

name: Mudslide
description: Matte, variegated brownish taupe glaze with an orangish brown stain. At Cone 6, the color transforms to gold/yellow that breaks over texture with enhanced mobility. Discontinued.

name: Cenote
description: Semi-opaque, sky blue gloss glaze that has a variety of brown crystals that melt during firing. At cone 10, the base color darkens and crystals become darker and more defined.

name: Oyster Shell
description: Glossy, pale gray glaze that breaks a darker gray. Semi-Transparent at 3 coats. At Cone 6, it transforms to opaque cream that breaks over texture and pools white. This glaze WILL move.

name: Rutile Wash
description: Washes are highly concentrated metallic glaze colorants for glazing and body staining. Cone 6 oxidation: Opaque deep rust red with slight sheen. Cone 10 reduction: Darkens. Cone 06 oxidation: Opaque mustard yellow with dry finish. Discontinued.

name: Frosted Lemon
description: Semi-opaque gloss yellow glaze that displays shades of yellow and white. Cone 6: Yellow and white variation. Cone 10: Light pink/purple.

name: Sand & Sea
description: Semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of light green and gold. Thicker application yields homogenous color; thinner areas break translucent with darker pooling around textures. Reduction at Cone 10 shifts to cream/gray/brown.

name: Light Flux
description: Flux is a flowing glaze developed for use with other stoneware glazes to enhance the movement, promote reactions and interesting effects. Appears as a translucent cream color when used alone. Exhibits increased movement at cone 10.

name: Wheat
description: Glossy, mustard yellow with an orangish brown stain at Cone 06. At Cone 6, it becomes semi-translucent, variegated gold/cream/brown that breaks over texture and pools cream.

name: Rose Granite
description: Glossy, orangish pink glaze that breaks dark pink at Cone 06. At Cone 6, it becomes semi-translucent red/brown that breaks over texture and pools blue.

name: Desert Dusk
description: A warm amber matte glaze with crystals that melt into a glossy purple and blue. Heavier crystal application will produce more color variation. Cone 10 reduction shows more prominent glossy purple/blue tones.

name: Raspberry Mist
description: A gloss, semi-opaque glaze that displays shades of dark and light pink. Thicker applications produce more uniform color, while thinner applications appear brighter and more translucent. The glaze darkens to deep red in reduction firing.

name: Amber Quartz
description: A warm amber matte glaze that adds accents of tan and brown. The variation of the glaze is dependent on application. Thicker applications yield more uniform color, while thinner areas break translucent. Cone 10 reduction: glossy purple/blue more prominent.

name: Macadamia
description: Creamy vanilla matte glaze that adds accents of gold. A stoneware matte that varies based on application thickness, breaking over texture with possible glossy blue pooling. Cone 10 reduction: variegated gray/cream.

name: Slate
description: Glossy, mottled dark and light gray glaze with a dark brown stain at Cone 06. Changes to semi-translucent variegated brown/blue at Cone 6, with enhanced mobility.

name: Glowing Embers
description: Matte, variegated brownish taupe glaze with an orangish brown stain featuring small and medium tan crystals. At Cone 6 oxidation: gold/brown base with brown crystals, enhanced mobility. Discontinued.

name: Coral Sands
description: A semi-opaque gloss glaze that displays shades of orange, yellow, and pink. Application thickness affects color homogeneity; thinner areas break translucent and pool darker around textures. Cone 10 reduction shifts to pink/brown.

name: Coral Puff
description: A translucent pink crystal glaze with white and blue/green crystals and blue specks. At higher temperatures, the base fades and crystals melt with potential surface issues. Discontinued.

name: Smoke & Fire
description: Translucent gray base glaze with white and orange crystals that melt/bloom and cobalt blue specks. Discontinued.

name: Lavender Sprigs
description: Semi-translucent, light gray base glaze with a variety of purple/brown crystals that melt. Glass frit pieces burst into colorful patterns during firing. Discontinued.

name: Field & Flowers
description: Opaque green base glaze with a variety of red, orange, black, and brown crystals. Glass frit pieces burst into color during firing. Discontinued.

name: White Cascade
description: Pottery Cascade are moving, flowing glazes, commonly used under or over other glazes to create reactions and movement. This glaze has minimal movement and maintains applied patterns while introducing white tones to other colors.

name: Black Metallic
description: Highly reflective, dark metallic finish following proper Raku Firing. Results vary due to the unpredictable nature of the firing technique. Not dinnerware safe.

name: Copper Metallic
description: Develops a glossy copper color with various iridescent shades. Due to the unpredictable nature of this firing technique, results will vary with every firing. Not dinnerware safe.

name: White Crackle
description: Develops a fine crackle pattern over a white gloss surface following a proper Raku Firing. Results vary due to the unpredictable nature of the technique. Not dinnerware safe.

name: Copper Penny
description: Develops a bright glossy copper finish with various iridescent shades of blue and purple following a proper raku firing. Results vary due to the unpredictable nature of the firing technique. Not dinnerware safe.

name: Peacock Matte
description: Develops a matte copper color with various iridescent shades of blue and purple following a proper raku firing. Results vary due to the unpredictable nature of the firing technique. Not dinnerware safe.

name: Oil Slick
description: Develops a mix of dark blue, green, and purple tones following a proper raku firing. Results vary due to the unpredictable nature of the firing technique. Not dinnerware safe.

name: Crystal Clear Brushing
description: Preserve and enhance the surface of your work. Note: may not be compatible with all Red Clays as a blueish haze may appear after firing. Cone 06: clean, clear surface and gloss finish. Cone 6: blistering/pinholing can occur.

name: Clear One Brushing
description: A fast-drying, gloss-finish brushing clear glaze that preserves and enhances the surface of your work. Compatible with Chrome Tin pigments and luster. Dinnerware safe.

name: Matte Transparent
description: Matte Transparent clear glaze forms an impervious barrier that protects against liquids, dirt, and abrasion. At Cone 06, the finish is matte. At Cone 6, it becomes glossy. Two coats recommended for Cone 06; one coat for Cone 6.

name: Pure Brilliance Clear Brushing
description: A high-performance gloss clear glaze that provides a sparkling protective finish with fast and stable dry time and craze-resistant properties. Works over various underglazes including Fundamentals and EZ Stroke.

name: Leaf Green
description: Glossy, dark yellow-green glaze that breaks a darker green. Semi-Transparent at 3 coats. Darkens at Cone 6. Not dinnerware safe. Discontinued.

name: Sea Mist Green
description: Sea Mist is a glossy, pale blueish teal green glaze that breaks a darker, blueish green. Semi-transparent at 3 coats. Not dinnerware safe. Discontinued.

name: Lavender Flower
description: Glossy, rich purple glaze that breaks blue at Cone 06 oxidation. This glaze is mobile and requires careful application on vertical pieces to prevent run-off.

name: Autumn
description: Glossy, variegated yellowish orange glaze with an orangish brown stain. Semi-transparent at three coats.

name: Turtle Shell
description: A glossy, greenish teal glaze that breaks dark greenish teal at three coats at cone 06. At cone 6, it transforms into a semi-translucent, variegated olive green/blue that pools darker over textured areas.

name: SPS Color Pop Glaze Kit- Midrange Glaze Cone 4-6
Orange
description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Reduction Look numbered 600-699 These glazes are formulated to mimic a gas or wood fired look. They flow and break into secondary colors, creating a dynamic effect on any clay body and when layering over other Opulence Glaze lines. By overlapping any Reduction Look 600 series over any EnviroColor 800 series glaze, you can achieve nice flow and movement. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 Reduction Look glaze colors by mixing stains or raw colorants to: 611 Eggshell. Opulence Glazes lines are compatible and may be blended by volume or weight to create an infinite variety of colors, shades and effects. Example: equal parts 605 and 600 make a nice flow blue. Of course, we recommend testing any combination to assure good results. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. Although Opulence Glazes were designed for C/6 electric firing, customers have reported successful results in gas or wood kiln as well as at various temperatures and conditions. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results.
