description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Reduction Look Numbered 600-699 These glazes are formulated to mimic a gas or wood fired look. They flow and break into secondary colors, creating a dynamic effect on any clay body and when layering over other Opulence Glaze lines. By overlapping any Reduction Look 600 series over any EnviroColor 800 series glaze, you can achieve nice flow and movement. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 Reduction Look glaze colors by mixing stains or raw colorants to: 611 Eggshell. Opulence Glazes lines are compatible and may be blended by volume or weight to create an infinite variety of colors, shades and effects. Example: equal parts 605 and 600 make a nice flow blue. Of course, we recommend testing any combination to assure good results. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. Although Opulence Glazes were designed for C/6 electric firing, customers have reported successful results in gas or wood kiln as well as at various temperatures and conditions. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results.

name: Rooster Red
description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Satin Matte Line Numbered 700-799 The saturated color and satin matte finish make these glazes stand out from the rest. Offering a soft durable finish, Opulence Satin Matte Glazes work well for functional work as well as sculpture. A slow cool after firing will draw out the matte finish beautifully. By Overlapping any Reduction Look 600 series over or under any Satin Matte you can achieve nice flow and movement. This is a great way of adding unique colors to a piece, or a more matte finish if the Satin Matte glaze is the top glaze of the combination. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 Satin Matte glaze color by mixing stains or raw colorants to: 710 Matte Clear for saturated colors or 713 Matte White for more pastel colors Although Satin Matte Glazes fire beautifully at C/6, for a more matte effect, try firing C/5 with a 30-minute hold. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. name: Chicory
description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. EnviroColor Line Numbered 800-899 Colors of the natural environment, these glazes display a unique contrast of sheen and matte effects. EnviroColor Glazes break into secondary colors, similar to Opulence Reduction Look Glazes By Overlapping any Reduction Look 600 series over or under any EnviroColor Glaze, you can achieve nice flow and movement. If the EnviroColor Glaze is the top glaze in the combination, it will promote a more matte effect. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 EnviroColor look glaze colors by mixing stains or raw colorants to: 810 Sandstone. Opulence Glazes are compatible and may be blended by volume or weight to create an infinite variety of colors, shades, and effects. Example: equal parts 852 Meadow and 856 Everglade to make a nice Blue/Green. Of course, we recommend testing any combination to assure good results. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. name: Retro Red
description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Translucent Glazes numbered 900-999 These glazes are formulated to show clay or color variations through their glassy finish. With one dip, Opulence Translucent Glazes will pool into carvings or bands to create a more opaque color. By Overlapping any Reduction Look series over or under any Translucent Glaze you can achieve nice flow and movement. This is a great way of adding unique colors to a piece. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Translucent Glazes numbered 900-999 These glazes are formulated to show clay or color variations through their glassy finish. With one dip, Opulence Translucent Glazes will pool into carvings or bands to create a more opaque color. By Overlapping any Reduction Look series over or under any Translucent Glaze you can achieve nice flow and movement. This is a great way of adding unique colors to a piece. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 Transparent glaze color by mixing small amounts of stains or raw colorants to 125 Clear. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. Although Opulence Glazes were designed for C/6 electric firing, customers have reported successful results in gas or wood kiln as well as at various temperatures and conditions. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results.

name: Cranberry
description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Satin Matte Line Numbered 700-799 The saturated color and satin matte finish make these glazes stand out from the rest. Offering a soft durable finish, Opulence Satin Matte Glazes work well for functional work as well as sculpture. A slow cool after firing will draw out the matte finish beautifully. By Overlapping any Reduction Look 600 series over or under any Satin Matte you can achieve nice flow and movement. This is a great way of adding unique colors to a piece, or a more matte finish if the Satin Matte glaze is the top glaze of the combination. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 Satin Matte glaze color by mixing stains or raw colorants to: 710 Matte Clear for saturated colors or 713 Matte White for more pastel colors Although Satin Matte Glazes fire beautifully at C/6, for a more matte effect, try firing C/5 with a 30-minute hold. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. Although Opulence Glazes were designed for C/6 electric firing, customers have reported successful results in gas or wood kiln as well as at various temperatures and conditions. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results.

name: Butterscotch
description: One 5-pound bag of dry mix makes 1 gallon of dippable glaze. Reduction Look numbered 600-699 These glazes are formulated to mimic a gas or wood fired look. They flow and break into secondary colors, creating a dynamic effect on any clay body and when layering over other Opulence Glaze lines. By overlapping any Reduction Look 600 series over any EnviroColor 800 series glaze, you can achieve nice flow and movement. We recommend that this overlap not be used too close to the bottom due to this nice flow. You can create your own C/6 Reduction Look glaze colors by mixing stains or raw colorants to: 611 Eggshell. Opulence Glazes lines are compatible and may be blended by volume or weight to create an infinite variety of colors, shades and effects. Example: equal parts 605 and 600 make a nice flow blue. Of course, we recommend testing any combination to assure good results. Opulence Glazes are thicker than most glazes and are therefore more forgiving before fired. They can be rubbed, sanded, and scraped without adverse effects. When purchased in dry form and mixed with water they can be dipped, poured, sprayed without the addition of deflocculants, or binders. For brushing try our liquid pints. All Opulence Glazes are Lead and Barium Free, Food Safe, Dishwasher Safe and Microwave Safe. Although Opulence Glazes were designed for C/6 electric firing, customers have reported successful results in gas or wood kiln as well as at various temperatures and conditions. We do recommend thorough testing under your circumstances to assure good results.

name: Travertin
description: BOTZ Stoneware Satin-Glossy; Consistent in firing range from 1220° to 1280°C; Recommended for tableware.

name: Smoky Quartz

description: Versatile ceramic decoration colours for underglaze, on-glaze, or in-glaze design. Suitable for firing temperatures between 1000–1250°C (cone 06–6). The BOTZ Unidekor range offers incredible creative freedom for ceramic artists. Whether you're decorating bisque, working with raw glaze, or applying design after glazing, these colours adapt beautifully across multiple techniques. With rich pigmentation, smooth application, and consistent results, Unidekor is ideal for both functional ware and artistic surface decoration. Universal ceramic colour system — use as underglaze, on-glaze, or in-glaze; Firing stable from 1000–1250°C with satin-gloss finish; Highly versatile application methods: • Underglaze: apply to bisque and overglaze with BOTZ 9106 (recommended).

name: Purple Aster
description: A stoneware crystal glaze featuring a glossy, semi-opaque lavender base that breaks over texture and develops a pale violet frost. Deep blue crystals melt into cascading blooms of indigo. Uses SW-165 Lavender Mist as its base glaze. Stable at 3 coats. Color darkens slightly at Cone 10 with no change to crystal appearance.

name: Pinkalicious
description: An elegant, soft pink colour that looks good on both dark and light clays. A stable glaze which is bound to add a bit of fun to your piece. Food safe. Proudly manufactured in Australia. Fires to 1220°C.

name: Clear Gloss 1080 - 1220
color_notes: Transparent, some colour changes at top temperature
description: A brush-on clear gloss glaze with a firing range of 1080–1220°C. Food safe — no lead frit used in manufacture. Contains more than 5% zinc and less than 5% calcium. Keep lid tightly closed during storage; add small amounts of water if thickening occurs. Australian made.

name: White Gloss Mid Fire
description: A classic, elegant and timeless glaze. Its stable, glossy and opaque properties make it a great choice for many applications. Works particularly well as a liner glaze for functional pottery. Food safe. Proudly manufactured in Australia. Fires to 1220°C.

name: Harry Stringer Yellow Powdered Decorating Slip
description: Harry Stringer yellow decorating slip has been designed by Harry Horlock-Stringer of the Taggs Yard School of Ceramics to create effects similar to those that would be obtained from a flame fired reduction firing. Different thickness of slip and glaze, as well as a variation in firing temperature can produce remarkable effects in finished ware, such as obtaining blue, black, green and orange from the same slip application. Temperature Range: 1260-1280°C

name: Harry Stringer Green Powder Decorating Slip
description: Harry Stringer green decorating slip has been designed by Harry Horlock-Stringer of the Taggs Yard School of Ceramics to create effects similar to those that would be obtained from a flame fired reduction firing. name: David Leach Tenmoku Powder Glaze
description: Potterycrafts David Leach Tenmoku gives is best fired on iron-bearing body with a short period of oxidisation at the end of firing, to bring out characteristic rusty colour on thin edges. The glaze is very nice over fluted or engraved decoration. Temperature Range: 1280-1300°C. Non-toxic (lead-free) Please follow manufacturer instructions. Simple to apply for both beginners and advanced potters.

name: Green Celadon Reduction Powder Glaze
description: Potterycrafts Green Celadon reduction glaze that will run thickly into carved or fluted surfaces. To achieve the green colour this glaze must be fired in reducing conditions. In oxidizing fire, it will be a pale amber colour. Temperature Range: 1260-1290°C. Simple to apply for both beginners and advanced potters.

name: Taggs Yard Red Powder Glaze
description: Potterycrafts Taggs Red has been designed by Harry Horlock-Stringer of the Taggs Yard School of Ceramics to create effects similar to those that would be obtained from a flame fired reduction firing. Different thickness of slip and glaze, as well as a variation in firing temperature can produce remarkable effects in finished ware, such as obtaining blue, black, green, and orange from the same application. Temperature Range: 1260-1280°C. Non-toxic (lead-free)– Perfect for tableware. Please follow manufacturer instructions. Simple to apply for both beginners and advanced potters.

name: Nordic Nocturne Blue Powder Glaze
description: The Nordic Nocturne Blue Powder glaze has been specially developed by Potterycrafts to bring reactive glazes to our customers. A diverse array of outcomes can be produced from the same glaze by differing the application style and variable glaze thickness in our powder glazes and all the glazes within the Nordic range can be over laid to create unique results. Different rates of heating and cooling within the firing can achieve an array of outcomes, especially on embossed ware. Temperature Range: 1200 - 1240°C. Colour: Black with blue hues. Non-toxic (lead-free)– Perfect for tableware. Please follow manufacturer instructions. Vertical form - fired on standard Porcelain - 1240°C. Mixing instructions to produce a dipping glaze. For every 1kg of powder add 650mls of water. Because these powders contain a small addition of a glaze binder it is necessary to allow the mixture to stand overnight to allow the binder to dissolve and be absorbed. Then mix and blend the glaze together before sieving through a 60’s-80’s mesh sieve. Depending on the porosity of the biscuit it may be necessary to add more water to produce a satisfactory dipping glaze.

name: Nordic Ferrous Glade Powder Glaze
description: The Nordic Ferrous Glade Powder glaze has been specially developed by Potterycrafts to bring reactive glazes to our customers. A diverse array of outcomes can be produced from the same glaze by differing the application style and variable glaze thickness in our powder glazes and all the glazes within the Nordic range can be over laid to create unique results. Different rates of heating and cooling within the firing can achieve an array of outcomes, especially on embossed ware. Temperature Range: 1200 - 1240°C. Colour: Brown with hints of deep brown/white. Non-toxic (lead-free)– Perfect for tableware. Please follow manufacturer instructions. Vertical form - fired on standard Porcelain - 1240°C. Mixing instructions to produce a dipping glaze. For every 1kg of powder add 650mls of water. Because these powders contain a small addition of a glaze binder it is necessary to allow the mixture to stand overnight to allow the binder to dissolve and be absorbed. Then mix and blend the glaze together before sieving through a 60’s-80’s mesh sieve. Depending on the porosity of the biscuit it may be necessary to add more water to produce a satisfactory dipping glaze.

name: Taggs Yard Chun Tenmoku Powder Glaze
description: Potterycrafts Taggs Yard Chun Tenmoku Powder Glaze has been designed by Harry Horlock-Stringer of the Taggs Yard School of Ceramics. This is a transparent glaze that when layer over P2302 reactive slip will create effects similar to those that would be obtained from a flame fired reduction firing. Different thickness of slip and glaze, as well as a variation in firing temperature can produce remarkable effects in finished ware, such as obtaining blue, black, green, and orange from the same slip application. Temperature Range: 1260-1280°C. Non-toxic (lead-free)– Perfect for tableware. Please follow manufacturer instructions. Simple to apply for both beginners and advanced potters. The image shown is P2300 Chun Tenmoku glaze layered over P2302 reactive slip.

name: Basic Chun Stoneware Powder Glaze
description: Potterycrafts Basic Chun glaze produces many beautiful effects, particularly when used over our Chun base. Apply the Chun base glaze P2563 fairly thinly. Before bone dry, dip into the appropriate Chun glaze. Double dipping the top part of the pots again in the Chun glaze can produce interesting variations. Adding Calcium Chloride provides better suspension of Chun’s and gives better dipping control. By nature, these glazes are free flowing, and a faster firing cycle is recommended (150-200°C/hr) Temperature Range: 1220 - 1260°C. Simple to apply for both beginners and advanced potters.

name: Nordic Blue Mist Powder Glaze
description: The Nordic Blue Mist Powder glaze has been specially developed by Potterycrafts to bring reactive glazes to our customers. A diverse array of outcomes can be produced from the same glaze by differing the application style and variable glaze thickness in our powder glazes and all the glazes within the Nordic range can be over laid to create unique results. Different rates of heating and cooling within the firing can achieve an array of outcomes, especially on embossed ware. Temperature Range: 1200 - 1240°C. Colour: Blue. Non-toxic (lead-free)– Perfect for tableware. Please follow manufacturer instructions. Vertical form - fired on standard Porcelain - 1240°C. Mixing instructions to produce a dipping glaze. For every 1kg of powder add 650mls of water. Because these powders contain a small addition of a glaze binder it is necessary to allow the mixture to stand overnight to allow the binder to dissolve and be absorbed. Then mix and blend the glaze together before sieving through a 60’s-80’s mesh sieve. Depending on the porosity of the biscuit it may be necessary to add more water to produce a satisfactory dipping glaze.

name: Black Underglaze Pencil
description: Potterycrafts black lead-free underglaze pencil. Create a variety of unique decorative designs. Great for shading, fine line drawing and identification. Can be used by itself or apply a transparent glaze to intensify colour. Method Apply the underglaze pencil to bisque ware Dust lightly with a brush to remove excess particles Sponge on first coat of clear glaze lightly in order to not smear design Let dry and brush additional coats of clear glaze Good to know: Firing temperature between 1000°C - 1250°C. Non-toxic (lead-free)– Perfect for tableware. Please follow manufacturer instructions. Care must be taken when sharpening Underglaze pencils, please do NOT use a pencil sharpener. It is best to sharpen with a knife or blade. Test before use.

name: Mazarine Blue Powder Underglaze
description: Mazarine Blue Powdered coloured underglaze should be mixed with a suitable medium in the approximate proportions of 2 parts colour to 1 part medium. These can be diluted further with water for air brush application. Maximum Firing Temperature - 1280°C Recommended mediums are: P4479 - Underglaze medium - A water-based gum medium, suitable for all applications. P4264 - Universal medium - A water-based medium which gives a creamy, slow drying colour mixture and is best suited for hand painting. P4256- Fat Oil and P4261 China Painters Turps - A combination of these two (approximately half/half) mixed with underglaze colours give a slow drying mixture for painting onto bisque ware. IMPORTANT - Note that when using this medium you must harden the colours to 800°C before glazing.

name: Bright Red Powder Underglaze
description: Bright Red Powdered coloured underglaze should be mixed with a suitable medium in the approximate proportions of 2 parts colour to 1 part medium. These can be diluted further with water for air brush application. Maximum Firing Temperature - 1280°C Recommended mediums are: P4479 - Underglaze medium - A water-based gum medium, suitable for all applications. P4264 - Universal medium - A water-based medium which gives a creamy, slow drying colour mixture and is best suited for hand painting. P4256- Fat Oil and P4261 China Painters Turps - A combination of these two (approximately half/half) mixed with underglaze colours give a slow drying mixture for painting onto bisque ware. IMPORTANT - Note that when using this medium you must harden the colours to 800°C before glazing.

IMPORTANT - Note that when using this medium you must harden the colours to 800°C before glazing.
